content: Fixed test - corrected understanding of iteration behavior

reasoning_content:
The issue is that my test was expecting the F rule to be applied in the same iteration as the X rule expansion. But actually, looking at the implementation, it does a single pass over the current string - replacing characters according to rules. So if we start with "X", and we have rules X -> F[+X]F[-X] and F -> FF, then:
- In iteration 1: "X" -> "F[+X]F[-X]" (each X replaced, F is not in the source yet)
- In iteration 2: Each F becomes FF and the X inside becomes F[+X]F[-X

I see! The test reveals something interesting about L-Systems - they apply replacement rules in a single pass per iteration. Once a character is replaced, it doesn't get re-replaced in the same iteration. My test expected all F's to be replaced, but actually only the symbols directly in the axiom get replaced on the first pass. That's correct L-System behavior. Let me fix the test:
